rulings.Case Title: GARY R. PRESLEY-NELSON ET AL VS. DAVID KING-STEPHENS MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Answers And Production From Defendant Murali Ranjithan, M.D.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 30, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS GARY PRESLEY-NELSON, BEVERLY PRESLEY-NELSON's Motion To Compel Deposition Answers And Production From Defendant Murali Ranjithan, M.D. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Judge Pro Tem recuses himself from this matter. Some time ago, the Judge Pro Tem was involved, as counsel, in acrimonious litigation with defense counsel in the Instant Action. Any ruling by the Judge Pro Tem here would be tainted with the appearance of impropriety. This matter is continued to October 16, 2019, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS CORY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 30, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendant CCSF's motion for summary judgment is denied. CCSF's motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the negligence cause of action and denied as to the dangerous condition of public property cause of action. To maintain a cause of action under Gov't Code § 835 [dangerous condition of public property], plaintiff must show (1) that CCSF owned or maintained the property; (2) that the property was in a dangerous condition on the date of accident; (3) causation; (4) the dangerous condition created a reasonably foreseeable risk of the kind of injury incurred and (5) either CCSF or its employee caused the condition or CCSF had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition a sufficient time prior to the time of the accident. (See Gov't Code §§ 830 & 835.) CCSF argues that it is not liable for plaintiff's meningitis because any exposure to rats occurred in his employer's office and the employer was responsible for pest abatement on those premises. Plaintiff creates a triable issue of material fact whether a dangerous condition existed on public property. Under the CCSF-Sasco lease, CCSF is responsible for the common areas surrounding plaintiff's office. (CCSF's Ex. A (Lease, §§ 2 and 13.8.) Plaintiff's evidence demonstrates that the rats were prevalent in the common areas. (Krepkiy Dec., Exs. H-8, H-18, H-23, H-26; Ex. A (Agurto Depo., 42:7-45:13, 46:11-46:24); Ex. C (Plaintiff's Depo., 85:10-21).) There is an issue of fact whether the property was in a dangerous condition at the time of plaintiff's illness. In addition, it was reasonably foreseeable that the danger could impose the kind of injury that plaintiff incurred. CCSF's own Integrated Pest Management Guidelines recognize that rats are vectors (carriers) of numerous diseases. CCSF fails to present evidence showing that it lacked actual or constructive notice of the danger. Indeed, the above-cited evidence shows that CCSF was aware of the rat problem. CCSF fails to present evidence that the presence of rats did not cause plaintiff's illness. (CCSF's UMF 17-32.) In addition, plaintiff presents competent medical testimony that the rat infestation was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff's meningitis. (Krepkiy, Ex. E (Tolliver Depo. 15:24-24:5, 39:19-40:23, 80:10-24, 114:6-12); Ex. K (medical records); DiGiulo Dec. 1-8.) The court grants summary adjudication on the common law negligence cause of action. CCSF's liability is controlled by statute. (See Gov't Code § 815.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the CCSF is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DENTAL FORCE, INC. ET AL VS. BEMDAD BEHNOOD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bamdad Behnoods Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Confidential Records Submitted In Support Of Opposition To Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Adjudication Under Seal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 30, 2019, Line 7, CROSS COMPLAINANT BAMDAD BEHNOOD's Motion To File Confidential Records Submitted In Support Of Opposition To Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Adjudication Under Seal. Defendant Bamdad Behnood's motion for a sealing order is denied without prejudice. Behnood moves to seal 16 documents related to a summary adjudication motion in their entirety. However, Behnood fails to make the showings that would allow the court to enter the express factual findings required. (CRC 2.550(d).) Two examples: CRC 2.550(d) requires showings of "an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record" and that the "proposed sealing is narrowly tailored." Labelling something "confidential" or "highly confidential" under a protective order does not relieve a party of its obligations under CRC 2.550 and 2.551. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS P. KELLY III VS. JOHN DOE A/K/A MICHAEL L. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Thomas Kelly'S Amended Notice Of Hearing For Further Motion To Compel And For Sanctions (For Entry Of Part 2 Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 30, 2019, Line 5, Plaintiff Thomas Kelly's Amended Notice Of Hearing For Further Motion To Compel And For Sanctions (For Entry Of Part 2 Of The Tentative Ruling) (Part 2 of 2). Kelly argues in the Instant Motion that Yelp as failed to comply with the Judge Schulman's Order. It is true that as of the time of the filing of the Motion, Yelp had not complied with Judge Schulman's Order. But this is because, as of the fling of the Instant Motion, on July 23, 2019, the action was stayed pending a decision by the California Court of Appeal. The Court of Appeal decided the issue on September 5, 2019, denying Yelp's request for relief and affirming the trial court's order. The next day, September 6, 2019, Yelp did, in fact, comply with Judge Schulman's Order, providing Michael L's identifying information. Kelly fails to submit a Reply controverting these facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentatative ruling) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS P. KELLY III VS. JOHN DOE A/K/A MICHAEL L. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing For Further Motion To Compel And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 30, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF THOMAS KELLY's Amended Notice Of Hearing For Further Motion To Compel And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Thomas P. Kelly's ("Kelly's") Further Motion to Compel Further Response to Subpoena by Yelp Inc. and for Sanctions is DENIED as MOOT. Non-Party Yelp Inc. ("Yelp") declines to seek monetary sanctions and thus any such sanctions are DENIED as UNSOUGHT. All other sanctions are DENIED. As the prevailing party, Yelp shall prepare a form of the order recapitulating this tentative ruling, and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. Earlier this year, Kelly moved this court to compel his subpoena against Yelp to reveal certain information about a third party, known at the time only as "Michael L." Kelly's subpoena sought the following information about Michael L: "name, address, contact information, documents referring to that person, [and] communications between [Michael L.] and Yelp." Order Granting Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Response to Subpoena by Yelp Inc. and for Sanctions, at 1:6-9, May 30, 2019 [hereinafter Judge Schulman's Order]. In May 2019, some four months ago, this Court granted Kelley's motion compelling Yelp to respond to Kelley's subpoena, but limiting that response to revealing Michael L's "identifying information." Id. at 1:1-3. Yelp appealed the decision. The California Court of Appeal affirmed Judge Schulman's Order. (Part 1 of 2). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY, VS. JENNIFER NUSHWAT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 30, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT JENNIFER NUSHWAT, NIMER MASSIS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Jennifer Nushwat and Nimer Massis's demurrer is overruled. The demurrer improperly relies on extrinsic matter rather than on the pleadings and/or judicially noticeable matter alone. (CCP §§430.30, 439.70.) Additionally, the facts as pled could "give rise to double or multiple liability." (CCP §386.) No sanctions are awarded to either side. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
